dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I remain rather partial to our old cauldron. This one that you procured with all the stars... your magick shan't just magickally improve because of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We still retain the old cauldron, of course. Just say the word and I can change it back! As for this one, a new beginning can yield powerful results, isn't that so?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, if this fancy bit of metalwork inspires you to greater acts, then 'tis worth its weight! I'll not ask you to change it back. And even if I did, heed your instincts, not mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: A properly imposing cauldron, that. Perhaps more suitable for certain specialties... daemonic invocations, curses, and the like. Do you intend to further specialize?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not necessarily, Headmistress. I... still have a lot of different ideas, sometimes several on a given night. It's my dream to pursue mastery in all dimensions of our craft.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Your dream, you say. We each have our leanings and yours perhaps shall manifest in time. But I do recognize the desire to avoid becoming limited, so practice as you will. Using this Python cauldron or whichever one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, there must be something we can do for Arachne. Her curse torments her. What use is our craft if we cannot ease a friend's suffering?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Arachne's fate is not yours to undo. 'Tis a matter between her and Athena. I suggest you leave it well alone.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How... did you know it was between her and Athena?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Can you help me discover who created Arachne's curse, if it wasn't Athena herself?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Why don't you ask her rather than me? I understand her hands are full, forestalling the destruction of Olympus... though surely she shall make some time for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Concerning your companion Arachne and her curse: Against my better judgment... I've done some searching, and have knowledge of the one who fashioned the poison.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You have? Excellent! Thank you, Headmistress! Who did you learn was responsible?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: That matter is solely between me and the spider. Utmost discretion is required in such matters. If you wish to be an intermediary still, then let her know to seek me here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress... you cursed my friend... poisoned her! You taught me never to be quick to judge, so help me understand...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Understand, then, that my allegiance forcibly is with the gods, not with arrogant mortals. You did not know the Arachne of old; she has been humbled in her current form.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then she has learned her lesson! Please help me change her back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I told you, 'tis a matter between her and Athena! For my part, I merely can express regret... to you, as well as her. Perhaps in time Athena herself may thus be swayed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The woods have eyes and ears, thus I'm aware Arachne and Athena finally settled up their differences. To the extent they ever shall, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Arachne always had her pride, but... I think she may have found some sort of peace.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Would that it were so. With time, feelings of scorn embed themselves deep in the flesh; in the heart. And they are not easily excised. But those two are stubborn enough to do it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Returning from the shadows restores your body, whilst meditating at your Altar emboldens your spirit. Such knowledge in the ashes of the past.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm still struggling to internalize the Arcana, Headmistress. If I share the same potential as my predecessors, why don't I feel a stronger bond?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Your bond can only be as strong as your understanding. One either learns from history or else repeats mistakes. I suggest the former.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You have been fixed upon your Altar even more than usual of late. Have the Arcana shown you aught of note?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm beginning to see the cards in a new light... each depicts a significant choice made in the past. Such knowledge may help make better choices in the future.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Indeed. We choose as wisely as we can, using what knowledge we have. So, you now clearer see into the cards, as you call them...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, I found Artemis out there. I'd not expected her so soon, but then, she's quick. Speaking of which, what of Hermes, any word?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Alas, not yet. However, those two are resourceful and discreet... and Olympus remains none the wiser to our long-standing connection with them, if that is your concern.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My concern is their safety. May Hermes soon discover what exactly Chronos is scheming and return, though I know we can ill afford to wait.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The goddess of the hunt decided that the best use of her talents presently is to regale us with song! As you can hear, I managed to resist my inclination to forbid any such thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm grateful that you did. I know Artemis sings to clear her mind and calm her nerves, but I think all the Shades about this place appreciate it, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis not how I myself prefer to face adversity, though I am open to whichever techniques work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: So, the Arms of Night have revealed to you some of their former selves; or future selves, perhaps. Few have earned the right to bear such weapons. Much less take advantage of their different likenesses!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The various Aspects I've discovered... some of the bearers I know well. Medea, Artemis... they're all connected to you, aren't they?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis not as simple as you may presume... you bear a set of artifacts whose aim is to safeguard the Underworld. Each Aspect is of somebody who played a part in this, or shall.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The Shades reporting to Odysseus suggest Athena herself appeared before you here? I mean not here, but in the Underworld. Is that something I ought believe, or no?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You ought. Athena gave her Gorgon Amulet, with which she's able to locate me far from where she normally resides. I trust I haven't inconvenienced her too much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Athena cannot go wherever she may please, no matter how it seems. But now you clearly have a stronger bond with her. And she, in turn, has a means to watch you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: At last we are the Unseen I, and feared across the realms. I thought 'twould surely take another age...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It wasn't a quick process but we got there, yes. For the Unseen do not relent.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Apparently not. What shall you do now? Pursue some other nigh-impossible result?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, have you always been so fond of beasts? It seems you would do anything for each other.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Beasts... saddled with such an uncharitable name. Beasts are far more trusting than most any mortal or immortal I have met.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They make it seem so simple, don't they? To fully trust.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: One knows where she stands with beasts, whether she is loved by them or doomed to sate their hunger. Would that the gods were so dependable.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: That Pitch-Black Stone in our training grounds... I've sensed it ever since your incantation. The subtle influence of Chaos on this realm.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Our goals are intertwined. When I gaze upon the Stone, I begin to see different paths and possibilities in every facet. Some leading closer to our victory.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Those paths and possibilities are real, like as not; as are the splendors that await, should you indulge our primordial benefactor. There always is uncertainty where Chaos is concerned... yet I trust that Nyx's parent is an ally, not a threat.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I was curious to see if you would consecrate the Altar of Ashes on your own. It ought to be further attuned to you as a result; the secrets of the Arcana, nearer to your grasp.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Altar is a threshold through which we all pass, going to and from these Crossroads. A gateway to the past, present, and future. I know it isn't mine alone; it's ours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I must say, you've been putting our old cauldron to good use. I see and sense the changes in this place. Changes for all our betterment, not just your own.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A proper incantation is never to be used for selfish gain. Though, if I can help bring about positive change from which I benefit, isn't that selfish, still?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Discard such doubts as that. You are not thinking solely of yourself, thus you are not thinking selfishly. I've not taught you to neglect your needs, have I?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's strange, I... can't think of anything to cast of late. Here at the cauldron I mean. Has that ever happened to you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: How often do you see me casting at the cauldron? 'Tis a natural part of your advancement. More incantations shall come to you eventually, although perhaps not soon. I know this is difficult for you, but don't worry about it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Once you're quite finished with the cauldron for the eve, I may have need of it myself... protective charms to be refreshed, wards reinforced...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you've a certain incantation in mind, might I be able to assist? The Invocation of the Dead, Concealment in Night's Embrace, whichever...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You've your own matters to consider now, Melinoë. Though if you're here in the vicinity whilst I'm chanting, your voice is always welcome, joined with mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: At the edge of the Mourning Fields... I faced some sort of dark, infernal beast. Three monstrous canine maws, bellowing with fury and sorrow. It was my father's... my family's old dog... wasn't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Through Odysseus we have a few reports about a creature fitting that description, more or less, but... Cerberus was red in hue, as blood. Nevertheless, a stay within the Fields could have transformative effects. Tsk, wretched thing. Torn from its home.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He doesn't seem to know who I am. Whether because it's been too long, or there's too much on his mind. Minds? But I felt a flash of recognition. He isn't too far gone, is he...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The nature of a beast can never change. Like us, however, they at times must fight the Daemons that torment them... a battle in which you can assist! Trust your training, and perhaps he can yet shake the spell that's overtaken him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You've not escaped the Mourning Fields as yet. The beast guarding the entrance into Tartarus seems insurmountable. 'Tis the effect of the Fields themselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What hope I have begins to drain from me as soon as I set foot in there. I know it's just a trick of the mind, but...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Last night, Headmistress, I traversed a gate that led me to the depths of Chaos... where I met its host! I've heard so much from you of Nyx, yet somehow I never expected to meet her mother and father.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: An audience with Primordial Chaos... I'd not entirely expected that! Although given Nyx herself has not been seen since Chronos took the House of Hades, I am unsurprised. There is no fury like that of a parent bereft of their child. Does Chaos seem an ally to us, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, I saw him. Chronos! He was in the pathways beyond Erebus. I struck as soon as I realized who he was, but to no avail.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: He thinks himself clever, the arrogant wretch. So then he knows of you. What did he say?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He thanked the Fates that we had finally met, as though he'd been searching for me all this time... and told me to not make the same mistakes as my family.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Eject that poisoned memory from your mind; he knows nothing of the Fates' designs. I shall reinforce all our protective sorceries, and you: Fear not your enemy; slay him!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos was waiting for me again outside of Erebus. This time I recognized him right away, but I could do nothing against him...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Wily bastard. Arrogant to the last.... What did he say?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He thanked the Fates that we had finally met, as though he'd been searching for me all this time... and told me to not make the same mistakes as my family.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Eject that poisoned memory from your mind; he knows nothing of the Fates' designs. I shall reinforce all our protective sorceries, and you: Fear not your enemy; slay him!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, I saw him. Chronos! First beyond Erebus, in the guise of an old man. He then attempted to ensnare me in some vision out of time... the river of flame, everything ablaze...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Still your heart, Melinoë, you're safe. And you were able to return again, which means that all your training served you well.... So then... he realizes who you are?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So he claims. I broke free from his spell like it was second-nature, but... neither could I raise a hand against him. What must I do now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Nothing has changed; you're to confront him down where he resides. Where your mother and father rightly ought to be. But go with patience, not with haste. He may know something of your power now, yet not enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The influence of Chronos is much stronger in Oceanus than here. He attempted to ensnare me in a vision of another time. A field of flame.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Yet you were able to elude his grasp. An aspect of your condition we expected would come into play as you pushed closer to our foe. Continue with still-greater caution, nonetheless...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, I found him! Chronos! Deep within Tartarus, just as you said. I did everything as practiced and as planned, except... he was too strong.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Still your heart, Melinoë, you're safe. And you were able to return again, which means that all your training served you well.... So then... he realizes who you are?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He does. We've lost the element of surprise now, haven't we? Damn it... how could I fail?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Nothing has changed; you're to confront him down where he resides. Where your mother and father rightly ought to be. But go with patience, not with haste. He may know something of your power now, yet not enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I reached the House, Headmistress. Did everything as practiced and as planned, except... he was too strong.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: No, this is an excellent result! You are still here, having learned more of what it takes to reach our enemy, and what he can do to resist! Do you feel ready to continue your pursuit?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes. If I can reach him... I can take him.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Again I've failed to destroy the Titan, Headmistress. Night after night he thwarts me, expecting I'll eventually give in...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: But you've no such intent, correct? You cannot stop him, and neither has he been able to stop you. You are resistant to his power and he cannot bar you from your realm.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You make it sound as though I've beaten him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You're back...! Now tell me everything. You found your mark? What then?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I reached the edge of Tartarus and made my way into the House, just as we planned. Chronos was there, on my father's throne. We clashed. He lost. But I know he isn't gone. And I was unable to linger there for long.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust you made each moment count. We were never going to destroy the Titan with brute force alone, but soon enough, he may yet be undone entirely. Provided you have more good tidings to report...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Mirror was intact... abandoned in the Prince's chambers. And the incantation worked! I found my brother in the past and spoke to him, but had so little time. I barely managed to convince him as to who I am.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Then speak swiftly the next opportunity you get. And continue not to overstay, lest the task end in failure. As for the Titan... study him, as I shall study what you found.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, by the way... something awaits you in the training grounds. You proved beyond a doubt that you're prepared.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You brought the Titan low again, but what transpired after that? Was your bygone brother more cooperative than before?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He doesn't know where to find Chronos, though seems willing to investigate. Perhaps he'll turn something up. I still scarce believe it's truly his past self I've spoken to...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Imagine how he feels. Continue to check up on him, but not a word of this to anybody else. Time flows ever forward, and we are not meant to travel opposite that stream.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: We heard about the Titan's latest misfortune by your hand. Is all else proceeding as intended, by your estimation?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think so. We just need to find a suitable weapon now, and then... everything that's come to pass can be undone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps not everything, but plenty. You know the rest by heart and I'll not pry for further details. But should an unexpected setback come to pass, tell me at once.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My father's spear, Gigaros... it was there in the Prince's chambers for me to find. Zagreus must have gotten access to it in his time, as I have now. One final step, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Not quite. We have one opportunity to use it against Chronos, and shall take every precaution to ensure it shall have the intended result.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You wish for me to turn my attention to Olympus for a while, so that we can test the Temporal Spear there first?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My father's spear, Gigaros... it was there in the Prince's chambers for me to find. Zagreus must have gotten access to it in his time, as I have now. One final step, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Not quite. We have one opportunity to use it against Chronos, and shall take every precaution to ensure it shall have the intended result. Perhaps whatever ails your Olympian relatives shall provide a suitable target.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You wish for me to turn my attention to Olympus for a while, so that we can test the Temporal Spear there first?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I found Circe the Enchantress on my route toward Olympus; or likely, she found me. She hasn't changed a bit from what I recall. You have her keeping watch over the seas?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: That I do. Selene takes to water like a cat, whilst Circe hails from the sea. Thus has it been a good arrangement for a while. I trust Circe's work retains its potency?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: We have a bit of a personnel problem with our Circe the Enchantress. Her isle is poised to survey our enemy's fleet, reports of which ought go directly to Odysseus. Except they go to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A gap in communication. By your leave, Headmistress, I can notify her of the proper protocol when next we meet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You shall not speak of this to her at all. I raise the point so you can be aware. Circe has long been a valuable asset and ally. Your visits to her isle may help keep it that way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Have you had opportunity to glean much from the Book of Shadows yet, Melinoë? Our task shall take you far beyond these Crossroads; you've much to learn as yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: As I begin to have insights into my experiences, the Book reveals more. Are they your teachings within, Headmistress?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Book of Shadows... I wasn't always certain who the author was. I suspected it was you, the way it sometimes anticipated my own thoughts before they were fully formed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You weren't entirely correct, though were on to something as to why the author understood your thoughts so well. 'Tis quite an old book... do take care of it for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: There's that faint line on your brow betraying your frustrations, Melinoë. I too can sense them in your heart. Sound them out, and rid yourself of them.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I was just thinking about curses. To be turned into a spider... forced to repeat everything you hear... stuck forever staring at your own reflection. Craft such as ours can inflict such ailments, yet cannot undo them?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You refer to the specific and imaginative afflictions of certain comrades of yours. Tell me, do they suffer still? Or have you aided them to the best of your ability, without relying solely on our craft?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Surely there's more I could have done for them, had I not restrained myself from using my abilities to the fullest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You could have acted negligently, yes. Instead you acted with restraint. Is that not for the best in matters concerning those for whom we care? You helped them cease being defined by their curses, and gave them new strength.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How can you be so certain? I haven't even said whom I'm referring to.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Melinoë, I can figure it out. And I likewise know your comrades are grateful to you for all that you've done.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You say such things to alleviate my mood.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I must commend you, Melinoë... helping our Tactician come to grips with his old connections, Arachne with her curse, that Shade-follower of yours with her memories, and more. You could have focused solely on your task.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Would you not have preferred that, Headmistress? As I was trying to aid my comrades, I sometimes couldn't shake the sense that I was doing something wrong. What's more, I couldn't even solve their problems. Not entirely...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: There are such problems that take root deeply enough that they become a part of us. These cannot be solved, so much as accepted and understood. Your comrades may never have achieved this without you, at least not anytime soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lost souls must first accept that they are lost, you used to say. That's true of more than just the Shades of Erebus, though, isn't it...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Correct. You are the Princess of the Underworld, and you possess a gift. 'Tis not your magickal acuity, for that you learned painstakingly. Rather, you inspire others to believe in themselves... as well as you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have strong-willed comrades... I hardly think that I've inspired them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You are a goddess. Do not diminish your accomplishments, especially to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: These Crossroads flourish thanks to this renewal project of yours. You saw an opportunity to get it off the ground even now?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This is our home. It should be cared for, clean, and beautiful. I recruited Dora to assist me so that the work wouldn't have to wait forever.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Dora? I've not once heard the invocation of her name and work in the same breath. To think that you persuaded that one to be of service.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Grow not attached to such a place as this; you belong within the luxury of your great father's House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You incanted the Return of Latent Memories on that Shade companion who yet haunts your residence. You were certain she desired this, and was prepared to face the truth?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I had seldom seen Dora want for something more, and she has a strong spirit. Wait, were you aware of her past?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I held to my suspicions, but 'twas not for me to speak of it. The complications between gods and mortals started long ago, and now cannot be easily untangled, if at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Am I supposed to just accept that my relatives may have been so careless or cruel with all these beings who look up to them? Perhaps Prometheus is right about us gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Prometheus makes sweeping condemnations. Live long enough, Melinoë, and you too shall make decisions you regret. Dora certainly has! Some of your relatives surely have, as well. All we can do is learn, and move on if we can.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You said some of my relatives. You think others may be more like Dora was... oblivious to the mistakes of the past?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I think they could not dwell on it, especially of late, with harder lessons from the past taking precedence. Perhaps at some point, you shall help your relatives make peace with all of this. When they are ready to face their own truth.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Would it be foolish and naive for me to try?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Eris has returned to us, I trust you've seen? 'Twas my dearest hope that leaving all her rubbish here would soon enough inspire her to pick it up, but alas.... You stay away from her.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I thought perhaps you'd finally relieved her of her entry privileges. I won't let Eris turn this place into a sty for pigs, even if it means having to clean up after her myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, how she'd chortle could she see now the frustration on your face. I know, ignoring things is seldom how one makes them go away, but... when it comes to Eris, I fear it's our best shot...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Sister Selene notified me it was Eris herself who barred you from gaining the heights of Olympus. She never should have got her hands on the Rail of Adamant again, that wretch.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, is there truly nothing we can do? Your oath to Nyx means Eris can come and go here as she pleases, only to try and blast me to bits when I approach my goal?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: One must carefully consider the terms of binding contracts, Melinoë. A mistake on my part you are paying for firsthand, heh. And I apologize. Do bring her hell when next you meet?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: That indolent little amphibian whom you keep... did my eyes deceive, or did I see him hopping rather energetically toward the egress?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You saw it true! I've compelled little Frinos to join me in my attempts out there. Surely you've taught me wards and charms enough to keep a Familiar safe?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: One can but hope! And may your Familiar keep you safe, in turn. And perhaps scrounge up some more roots and such for us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: A cat recently strutted clear into our grove, as though my wards of shadow weren't even there! Please tell me that's a new Familiar of yours?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That would be Toula, Headmistress, and yes! I figured I could use a confident and independent companion such as that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: As can we all. The beasts whom you entrust are welcome here. Would that all our acquaintances were such a welcome sight...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How fare Hecuba and Gale lately, Headmistress? They must be ill-at-ease with all of the bustle and new faces and the like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, they're fine. The hound and polecat make strange bedfellows, but are able to withstand more than their share of hardship or discomfort. Especially when you lavish them with attention!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You always reminded me of your father, Melinoë, but lately you remind me of your mother... more and more. The concern for others that you sometimes show... it can be an exploitable weakness.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I would never put any such concerns ahead of the task, Headmistress.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: See that you don't! Although my remark was not intended as admonishment; it was my honor to have known your family... even if merely from the shadows.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Vengeance for your family awaits you, far below. I'm certain they would be proud of what you have become. Bear them in your heart when you strike.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, I'll bear them in my heart but... I have no memory of them. How could I? You're the one who's always been there for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Augh, don't say such things; I am not your mother, Melinoë. So much she could have taught you, I cannot. Never compare us.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The family portrait in my tent... I have been thinking on it more and more, I... never asked you where you got it from.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Twould be stranger if you did, for the answer ought be obvious to you. Though, you would know the circumstances?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, Headmistress, if you please. I know it was a time you would never wish to revisit, but it's important to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Of that I have no doubt. When Chronos stormed your father's House, I fled with but three things: Hypnos, because he was there; that family portrait, which pulled at my heart; and you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I dreamt of Father once again. When Chronos came for him, and you took me to safety. Except this time, I remained there longer than before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis more than a dream, of course. And what else did you learn whilst you lingered thus?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I learned Chronos wanted for Father to tell him where to locate the Three Fates. Chronos hates them, or feels threatened by them. He's been searching for them all this time, hasn't he? And finally found them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Which means they may not take our side if forced to choose. I feared as much, yet didn't wish to lend the possibility my voice. Well, this certainly fits the spirit of the news swift Hermes relayed when he spurred us to act! Something real real bad, I believe were his words?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Cultivating all those many different types of greenery. We are in the realm of the dead, lest you forget, Melinoë! Yet here you tend your garden.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No better place to grow reagents above or below. But more than that, it's something small I can look forward to whenever I return. Helps keep me centered.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: There's no need to explain. The Earth obliges you; 'tis wise to take advantage of good fortunes such as that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I found him, Headmistress! My father! Chronos has him locked away in Tartarus. But, I couldn't save him... he asked I leave him be...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Truly? How utterly predictable of Chronos to gloat in such a way. I regret that your reunion with Lord Hades thus transpired... though, I am gladdened you discovered him. How does he fare?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He... ungh. I really wouldn't know. He seemed sound of mind and body, and he knew me soon enough. Gave me what blessing he could still bestow. What can we do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: If he asked you leave him be, then 'tis evidence he wishes to remain unseen. Use full discretion and be brief, should you encounter him again; and I shall see about ensuring Chronos remains ignorant of your newfound connection.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I ought have told you by now but I found him, Headmistress... my father. Chronos has him locked in Tartarus like a common prisoner... he asked I leave him be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Truly? How utterly predictable of Chronos to gloat in such a way. I regret that your reunion with Lord Hades thus transpired... though, I am gladdened you discovered him. How does he fare?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He... ungh. I really wouldn't know. He seemed sound of mind and body, and he knew me soon enough. Gave me what blessing he could still bestow. What can we do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: If he asked you leave him be, then 'tis evidence he wishes to remain unseen. Use full discretion and be brief, should you encounter him again; and I shall see about ensuring Chronos remains ignorant of your newfound connection.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, I've a message from my father that he asked me to relay: He sends his deepest gratitude...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Lord Hades told you this? Whyever for? He rots in his own prison whilst the Titan ransacks his domain! My charge was to safeguard the realm and I did not.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Father spoke of when he left me in your care, when Chronos struck. He must have feared the worst. Now he knows I'm well... all thanks to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: No, Melinoë, all this is thanks to Chronos. Your father would do well to save his gratitude ere we settle certain scores. Though... speak not to him of this... undignified response. I merely was taken aback.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hermes is back, Headmistress. He was able to lend some of his swiftness, and... also asked that I come to the aid of Olympus. Has the hour grown so desperate up there?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: He would not ask otherwise. To be sure, this complicates the task. My view, however, is that you are not yet prepared for the surface. I'll not lift the wards barring that path.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And if I were to lift them myself? I've seen you do it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Hypnos certainly left in a hurry. It took you longer to wake him than I initially surmised, but 'twas for the best.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What, you knew this would happen? Why didn't you say anything for all the times I tried to break him out of his slumber? I thought you were ignoring him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I was, after I saw that you had learned sufficiently. Your affinity toward dreams and the like proved necessary to the task, and Hypnos, in his way, was an accessory to that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You saw that Hypnos brought something for you? A special order from the House, so I was notified. And I don't mean that little pile yonder where he used to doze.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have! At first I thought it was a place of rest there in the training grounds. But it's a means by which I can connect more deeply to my dreams... to my worries and such.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Melinoë, the point of resting is to set your worries well aside. But I know some are inescapable, and if this is a means for you to still these restless corners of your mind, then so be it. Do take what time you wish with that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, I saw Icarus up there, during my voyage toward Olympus. He flies free, but fights for us still... doing what he can to disrupt the Titan's legions.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: And...? You need not make attempts to endear him to me. My judgment of one's character need not affect your own.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I just... sometimes I worry you think he abandoned us during a time of need, and wasn't a good influence on me...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You need not speculate about my thoughts. The follies of young Icarus are positively mild in comparison to most. I harbor no ill will toward him; he'd not be flying if I did.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd not expected Lady Medea to be stationed in Ephyra still, Headmistress. To think even a witch of her experience was unable to prevent the city's fall.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Twas not her responsibility to try. Medea was there to blend with the locals. It seems she's even more successful at it now, with most of them deceased. I trust she aided you in her own way?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: How fares Medea on the surface lately from your point of view? Having a grand old time by her account, and Sister Selene seems to think the same! Perhaps I should not be surprised.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Medea does seem very much at home amid all the death and decay, which fuels her research. She's always toiling at one curse or another. Is that a concern?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: My concern is only for her well-being, and it sounds as though she is all right for now. So long as her research does not come back to haunt her anytime soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Medea notified me that the two of you have had your little altercations recently but that I ought not to be concerned. 'Twas apparently the will of Night that brought it forth!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Medea speaks true, though I'd not expected her to side with the Cyclops Polyphemus in these altercations of ours. You didn't order her to test me, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I ordered her to heed the Oath, and it appears she has. Well, 'tis a good way to bring out her enthusiasm for her work if nothing else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: One more of Nyx's kin is here for us to mind. Your incantation tracked him after all! Doom typically arrives uninvited, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Moros is to be our guest. I know his presence must seem an ill portent, but if the Fates wanted him here, who are we to fight it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This task... why does it fall solely to me, Headmistress? Because, Nemesis, she...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: She is a burden. Had I not sworn to Nyx that I would watch over her children, I'd have cast her out for insubordination long ago.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She's frustrated you haven't given her a chance. Her strength far surpasses mine and she's trained at least as hard; I've seen it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Why defend her thus, Melinoë? Her attitude speaks far louder than she. I shall entrust nothing of import to one such as that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Your dreams have troubled you again, I see. Despite your brave attempts to conceal it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can manage them, Headmistress, it's just... it's Chronos. I now hear him even when I'm still as death.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Then turn your nightmares into his. Your subconscious self is your greatest refuge. Let none there remain whom you do not permit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis fortunate your father's realm is vast indeed, that you may circumnavigate it at the edge of Oceanus, where our enemy is relatively weak.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If we cannot pass directly through Elysium, then we shall make what way we can. Though the fiends that lurk the border of the sea care not for my presence.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: God-hating monsters with salt in their veins; the seas covering the world's surface just were not enough for them. The sunken chambers they occupy have not yet fallen to Chronos, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I know he thanked you personally though I wish to express my gratitude in turn that you were able to assist our Tactician with matters that weighed on him. I suppose all of us here are haunted by our past in some way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus did all the work. I merely arranged a few meetings where distance would have been a factor. Though, I know it's best to avoid using our craft in such matters.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: If you did not use our craft to sway the outcomes of these meetings, then you did not overstep. For his part, Odysseus has seemed more at ease, having finally confronted those unresolved troubles of his... and perhaps finally sailed past.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Our Tactician has decided to take his leave. Off to settle some of his personal affairs, he says. As good a time as any, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not used to seeing Odysseus away for long. He'll be back, won't he? He's always been so devoted to this place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus and I... long has one of us been certain you would find your way to Mount Olympus and direct your efforts there. The other was certain you'd do no such thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Before you even ask, there's absolutely no way you're making me guess which one of you was which in that particular disagreement, Headmistress.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I always said how you achieve the task is up to you. 'Tis not for me to guide your every step! Then was it worth it, climbing to those heights?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Were the heights of Olympus all that you imagined, at the Palace of Lord Zeus? If you're to overcome such hardships to reach that point, the view had best be worth the while.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The palace grounds were like nothing I've ever beheld. Though everything was so vivid, I scarce could see at all. I felt as though I didn't belong there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: That you do not. Well, as long as your mountain relatives showed you a bit of hospitality in the brief time you had!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Much is expected of you. The pressure that you feel; you never speak of it, and yet full well I know it's there. Your constant companion.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can hardly distinguish between pressure and faith anymore. My entire life has led to this, and all of you are counting on me. Are you truly so certain in my inevitable success, Headmistress?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Pressure and faith are intertwined, 'tis true. Ever in conflict. Should the pressure utterly prevail, out comes despair. Only then do we truly falter.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You didn't answer my question. The combined might of the gods was not enough to stop Chronos before. And you know the Fates are unpredictable. My success is not a certainty...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: My faith is not in outcomes, 'tis in you! And I've enough of it to last. Should you find yours lacking, I shall always spare you some of mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You flushed Prometheus out from his hiding-spot. Slipped from his chains somehow! Whether he's a pure idealist or merely in it for rebellion's sake, I've yet to ascertain.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: His reasons for siding with Chronos seem plain enough; for one, he wants his vengeance on Lord Uncle Zeus and the rest. And for another, he's a Titan himself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I am a Titaness as well, as is Sister Selene, lest you forget! Our lineage need not dictate our loyalties or principles. Although perhaps Prometheus sees otherwise.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The fire-thief Prometheus insists on barring your path toward the mountain summit still. What nonsense has he filled your head with recently?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He seems to bear a deep sense of responsibility and guilt for mortalkind. I'm concerned he aims to spread dissension in our ranks... some Shades who serve our cause must harbor sympathies.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: It sounds to me as though you are beginning to harbor such sympathies yourself. Do not be deceived by his lofty claims; he's sided with our enemy, and made that clear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Hermes informed me that Prometheus redoubled his attacks upon Olympus, after he fended you aside. Have I not trained you how to withstand flames?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If only all he had was flames, but no, he has a vicious Eagle pet and more. What of Olympus, what has he done?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: He's kept the gods on their back foot. Never fully committing his forces, avoiding fights he cannot win! Frustrating adversary, even if he shall not prevail.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: At least our cauldron's finally purified, praise the Fates! Speaking of which, are you as yet considering an invocation to them? I have my doubts that they would listen, even now...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If there's any chance the Three Fates shall aid us, I'll take it. Thank you for preparing things for me. Though, I'm well short of freshly picked reagents still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Mm. I'd offer such reagents if I could but, as you venture past this glade look carefully along your path and you shall find them. Fates willing...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates indulged you after all. That is their scroll beside your tent; it followed you out of the shadows. Perhaps it can provide some answers.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Would that it does. The Fated List has tasked me with ensuring certain minor prophecies are fulfilled, though says little else as yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: May it reward your efforts, at least. But do not expect the Fates to always favor you; the bane of our enemy is not necessarily our friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You have a flush about you I could see as you approached! No need to tell me of its source, if you prefer to keep whomever it is to yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Whomever it is, Headmistress? Could it not have been the product of a balmy night, or lingering effect of my return?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Unlikely. Look, Melinoë, I was much like you once. I've said to you before that feelings mustn't be ignored. Heed the ones that spur you on, they're good! Confront the rest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Have you been finding anything of use out there, in your forays? No point returning empty-handed. Though you may lack the necessary tools, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Erebus still teems with hidden life. I'm finding all sorts of things we need out there. I'm very grateful for the purse! It never weighs me down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: A witch cannot afford to be encumbered. The blessings and such from your relatives may be fleeting, but whatever goes into that purse isn't going anywhere. Not till you use it, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wanted to ask you something, Headmistress. All these reagents I've been gathering, taking from the Earth. From Mother Gaia. How can I make it up to her?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, you can start by not wallowing in senseless guilt. Think you that Gaia would reveal her abundances to you if she believed you would not put them to good use?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But you taught me never to take more than I need. Now I'm taking everything in sight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: And Gaia knows you'll need all that and more. Fear not, your plunder returns whence it came through your craft. If not your garden there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You're frustrated, Melinoë. Why can't I simply furnish you with all of the reagents you require? So many incantations you could sooner cast...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know full well the answer, Headmistress. Your own reagents bring power to your craft, as my own do to mine. Only through the effort of their procurement do they gain their properties.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Just so. At times 'tis quite the pain, admittedly. Yet what is our craft but making our intentions manifest? For which there can be no shortcuts.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The Tactician furnishes you with some of the reagents you require. Yet why not I? So many incantations you could sooner cast.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know full well the answer, Headmistress. Your own reagents bring power to your craft, as my own do to mine. Only through the effort of their procurement do they gain their properties.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Just so. At time 'tis quite the pain, admittedly. Yet what is our craft but making our intentions manifest? For which there can be no shortcuts.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: This all has come to be a comfortable routine. Each night you return refreshed; tireless. But I, at least, ought take a bit of rest.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think we have things under control lately, so please do. Will you be going anywhere?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Not yet. But I may be less talkative, as is my tendency.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Sister Selene tells me that she lent you her brilliance and might. You were able to draw her down even within this place?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I found a source of moonlight and invoked Selene's aid using the Words, Headmistress. I can't imagine the extent of her power.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You know her power perfectly well. You simply haven't had the opportunities to wield it. You shall have them very soon, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The Shades about this place are all abuzz in their support of you, you know. You have a gift for leaving an impression on them. And for compelling more of them to join with us.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They were just people once, or other living beings. All they ever needed was something to believe in, in death as well as life. And a way out of Erebus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Hm, well. They're sworn to you, that much is clear to me. I'd purge them all if I had any doubt! Perhaps they think they'll find a cushy spot within Elysium once all is said and done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Chronos must believe he has the upper hand now that Olympus has the Father of All Monsters bearing down on them night after night. But he has made a grave mistake...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Typhon cannot be controlled... if he no longer had Olympus to contend with, he would probably come after Chronos next. There must be some way we can redirect his rage.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: If not his rage, the very nature of his being... I have always taught you that our craft is never to be used for destruction alone. But, no rule is immutable. Let us use our enemies' strengths against them, to rid us of them all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Disintegration of Monstrosity is yours now to incant if you so choose, and yet you hesitate. Ought I to be concerned?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No more than we all should be of late, Headmistress. I wish to make additional preparations before committing to the final phase of the task. Or shall I rush headlong into it now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not be cheeky with me. 'Tis hardly the occasion to begin making light when you seldom have thus far. Get ready, then. As patiently as you must.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, what is that you and Odysseus are planning? He's always with his charts and diagrams.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Our Tactician always plans ahead, and is plotting a course from the nearest Underworld entrance all the way to Olympus, through the enemy's ranks... just in case.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: In case of what...? I can't go up there, and Chronos is the opposite way. Are matters on Olympus growing worse?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: We are awaiting confirmation about that. These are mere possibilities for now; distractions, even. Remain focused.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, if Olympus is besieged, then why am I not permitted on the surface? If I could aid the gods, then they could aid us even more!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Melinoë, however you complete the task is up to you; I have not barred you from the surface. But I caution, if you go, your strength shall fail you. Your birthright is to blame; no fault of yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Born in the Underworld and here I'll stay, thank the Fates. I don't intend to live up there, I just.... Do you know how long I'd even have?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I know your brother and father had a day at best ere they could not stand it any longer. Perhaps you're different, but don't count on it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You visited the surface. I can't imagine that played out particularly well. At least you made it back at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It was draining... even at night, something about being under the stars, I... didn't know there were so many. It was more than I could take. You were right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: A lesson learned, and an experience gained. The Fates themselves do not want you up there. What shall you do?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have an incantation to complete. I don't need to stay forever on the surface... I just need to hold on long enough to make it to Olympus...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, then you seek to improve this aspect of yourself, without rejecting it outright. I see how that may work...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You visited the surface. I can't imagine that played out particularly well. At least you made it back at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It was draining... even at night, something about being under the stars, I... didn't know there were so many. It was more than I could take. You were right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: A lesson learned, and an experience gained. The Fates themselves do not want you up there. What shall you do?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress... Olympus seems reluctant to ask outright, but I can tell my family there needs me. I cannot shake the sense that I have to help them, before I can achieve our task.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You needn't shake that sense, for it may well be accurate. Are you still thinking of an incantation that may help you to survive such a journey?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I am. Up there, I'll be like a fish out of water, right? Suffocating. But with a proper incantation, I was thinking maybe I could hold my breath. Until Olympus is secure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Such a spell shall not be simple. Though, if you demonstrate that you're sufficiently well traveled, then... perhaps magick such as that shall help you go farther still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Your recent travels to the surface. How were you able to survive up there? You were away for longer than I would have expected.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My surface-breathing incantation made a difference after all, Headmistress. I still felt the oppressive atmosphere up there, weighing down on me... but not crushing me quite like before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Magick born of your resolve. I'm impressed, though not entirely surprised. May your craft thus protect you ere Olympus is secure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: It must be very difficult for you up there. My aim was to prepare you for the dangers of the Underworld, not those Olympus ought be able to take care of on its own.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Every misstep on the surface is a harsher lesson even than below. I've become more used to it, but I'm constantly reminded I should not be able to set foot up there at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: And yet you carry on! Notice how I do not even attempt to cure you of that stubbornness; 'tis probably your family's most pervasive trait. Well, if you wish to keep making things even harder on yourself, at least you know the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: What troubles you, Melinoë? Your brow is furrowed not with fierceness but with doubt... the one emotion we cannot afford.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Ungh, Headmistress, it's just... I'm struggling to muster the rage I should feel. I'm tasked with slaying a Titan I do not even know, to avenge a family I cannot remember...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Your feelings never lie to you; 'tis their origins that may deceive. You feel no rage? Then you are in control. Tell me, do you question the need to complete our task?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, Headmistress. Treachery against the House of Hades cannot go unpunished.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: How fares the task thus far, Melinoë? Our enemy yet remains indisposed with more pressing matters. The time to strike at him is now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There is much left to do, Headmistress. You must know that. How much time do I have?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Who can say? Our enemy is Time itself. Time's passage seems to bear little meaning anymore. Do not rush needlessly. The task shall only take longer that way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: How fares the ever-sinking fleet of ships within the Rift of Thessaly? 'Tis a wonder Chronos gathered as many followers as he has, using such shoddily built craft.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The fleet shows no real sign of slowing down. Fortunately, the same sorcery that seems to be holding those ships together lets me transfer quickly from one to the next. Are all surface ships so odd-looking?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I certainly think not. These are merely emblematic of the Titan's false promises of a new age. They assault not just Olympus but our sensibilities as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Those Sands that you recovered from our enemy. They've something of his nature in each tiny grain. A key of sorts, to the Dissolution of Time. Do you concur?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I do. That very incantation sprang to mind... an expression of all you taught me of his weaknesses. A means to unravel his very being... to bring death to Chronos at last. He doesn't seem to realize it's possible... still thinks I'm just an agent of Olympus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis merely evidence that our concerted efforts have not been in vain. You cross the Underworld like a tempest, whilst I remain in shadows. But, when the Titan's time at last comes to an end, I dearly wish to be there myself. To see the look on his face.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I recall when I myself first grasped the Flames of Ygnium. I must have been about your age. Now you are possessed of their power.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All it takes is a bit of light to cast a long shadow; isn't that so, Headmistress? I'll do my best to wield the Flames as you have taught.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Selene indicates that you seemingly withstood the Vow of Rivals all the way to the end. She's not exactly prone to error, but such a claim I need to verify.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then consider the claim verified. I almost expected some great feast or ceremony considering all it took. Though I suppose that's not the way the will of Night is done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx herself would say, seek to be valuable, not to be known. Selene and I at least know what you achieved. But if 'tis glory you desire, you were born in the wrong realm.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The foul deed is done. Have no pity for that monster, only gratitude... for yielding his power in his absence. Now you are ready for the final phase.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Before I depart... my brother asked something I didn't know how to answer. If Chronos is destroyed before all this... what exactly happens to us here and now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us discover the truth of it together. I am certain only that this is not goodbye. The conclusion of your task is at hand. Go, end this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Has the thin air upon that mountain made you mad, Melinoë? Confronting Typhon of all creatures face to face! Hermes told me everything. Save what was going through your head.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was not about to turn my back on my family, especially not after having come so far. What should I have done, flee at the first sight of that monster? We flee only as a last resort.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: That we do, and you had best keep reminding yourself of it ere you confront the Father of All Monsters again. Do not take unnecessary risks, or lose sight of the task.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Has the thin air upon that mountain made you mad, Melinoë? Confronting Typhon of all creatures face to face! Hermes told me everything. Save what was going through your head.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He's much, much larger than any other foe I've faced, but that doesn't make him invincible. I suspected he'd have a difficult time contending with a small, quick adversary.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I would commend you for the outcome were I not so utterly aghast. The Father of All Monsters may not be quite so easy to surprise ere you confront him again. Do not take unnecessary risks or lose sight of the task.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis a wonder that your mountain relatives are still able to be of benefit to you whilst having Typhon at their gates. Do they have any sort of strategy to stop him, do you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They're working together to drive him back, along with the Titan's legions. But if they have a greater plan to put an end to the assault, they've not yet let on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us assume then that they've no such plan. However, I'm beginning to think the Father of All Monsters may yet figure into ours. Collect what samples that you can from him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis a wonder that your mountain relatives are still able to be of benefit to you whilst having Typhon at their gates. Do they have any sort of strategy to stop him, do you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They're working together to drive him back, along with the Titan's legions. But if they have a greater plan to put an end to the assault, they've not yet let on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us assume then that they've no such plan. However, I'm beginning to think the Father of All Monsters may yet figure into ours. Collect what samples that you can from him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Your victories against Typhon... have you there anything to show for it? Any sort of specimens or samples of his flesh?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I hope you like hideous eyes, Headmistress. Is there an incantation that you have in mind requiring such filth?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps. Hold onto all of that for now. But when the time is right, we shall turn all that monstrous power back against itself. And rid this world of that fiend for good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Your victory 'gainst Typhon... have you there anything to show for it? Any sort of specimens or samples of his flesh?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I hope you like hideous eyes, Headmistress. Is there an incantation that you have in mind requiring such filth?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps. Hold onto all of that for now. But when the time is right, we shall turn all that monstrous power back against itself. And rid this world of that fiend for good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Hermes indicated you were instrumental in releasing Typhon's grip on Mount Olympus recently. I trust that your embattled relatives contributed their share to that result?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I may be just a frontline diversion in stopping the monster's attacks, but it worked. Lord Zeus at least was able to remind Typhon about the power of his lightning bolt. The others had their hands full someplace else...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The gods must be grateful for such an effective frontline diversion, and Chronos must be grateful for the brief reprieve as well. But carry on as you see fit; this was all part of the Titan's plan, and shall likewise factor into ours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Each of the two great enemies we face, you have laid low, one after the other. Thus, we begin to take the upper hand...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I may be able to beat Chronos or Typhon in a fight, but at times I feel as though I've come no closer to stopping either of them. It's reassuring you think otherwise.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I more than think; you've demonstrated that you have the strength and knowledge to complete your task. As to whether you have the patience, that we shall have to see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Dissolution of Time. Disintegration of Monstrosity. Connected incantations, negating that which has already come to pass, and violently. A terrible use of our craft...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It is. You always said such power ought never to be used, save when no other possibility avails itself... perhaps not even then. How did you discover such incantations? You practically know more of Chronos than he does himself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: When I learned of what Chronos had done to you and the rest... I refused to accept it. But I studied him nevertheless. Power always contains the secrets of its own undoing.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Entropy is the last reagent we require for the Dissolution of Time, but... where am I supposed to find it? I've found no trace as yet... even from Typhon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Where are you supposed to create it is the correct question to be asking. A powerful enough force must be put entirely to rest. Do you catch my meaning?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My father's spear, Gigaros... it was there in the Prince's chambers for me to find. Zagreus must have gotten access to it in his time, as I have now. One final step, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Not quite. We have one opportunity to use it against Chronos, and shall take every precaution to ensure it shall have the intended result.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Entropy is the last reagent we require for the Dissolution of Time, but... where am I supposed to find it? I've found no trace as yet... even from Typhon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Where are you supposed to create it is the correct question to be asking. A powerful enough force must be put entirely to rest. Do you catch my meaning?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus and I finally reached an understanding. He knows what needs to be done... but, I wanted to ask you something, Headmistress. With Chronos, in the past... there's truly no other course but to destroy him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, blood and darkness, he's got to you, hasn't he? Your brother at least, if not Chronos himself. Why are you asking me such things when you have come so close to the completion of your task, Melinoë?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, for one... we're on the verge of destroying Time itself. For all you've taught me, I've never fully understood the repercussions this would have.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: We cannot know such repercussions ere they come to pass. Our charge, your charge, is to stop Chronos. Whatever it takes. I trust you shall require no additional reminders?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I commend your singular focus in dealing with Chronos in your father's House... however, I must admit the matter facing your mountain relatives can no longer be ignored.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, you're suggesting I should shift my attention to the surface, when we're this close to victory?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: We're not as close to victory as you surmise. For one, the Dissolution of Time requires a key reagent you do not yet have. 'Tis not something you'll find below; I realize that now, and so should you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The Nocturnal Arms are beginning to awaken. May we yet see an age when they can slumber once again. Have you a favored sister yet among the lot?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I am grateful that the weapons of Night have accepted me. I intend to treat them equally, Headmistress.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: A commendable approach, but not a necessary one. The Nocturnal Arms share our purpose. They care little as to which of them you use, only that the task is completed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You look unwell. Returning is difficult, I know. Tell me if you need anything.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think I'm all right, thank you, Headmistress. How do you manage it when you are out of sorts?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I've a draught for every mood, and more. Sorrow, pain, anxiety, love-sickness, dispassion, rage, fear, nausea, cowardice... all sensations one can rectify, whether with mental fortitude or, failing that, a potent drink.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: With all your incanting as of late, I must needs caution you to not overextend. I know you want for every chant and glamour on the tip of your tongue, but they require space, and time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Using the cauldron doesn't drain me as once it did. Though I know I mustn't be reckless.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Indeed, lest your fair right hand come to match your left. Although perhaps that boy-shade Icarus would fancy you more if that were so. Been gone awhile, hasn't he?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, Lord Zeus requested prompt reports, his words, from you about the situation in the Underworld. He seemed a little frustrated. Asked me to let you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: And you're as dutiful as ever, aren't you? Exactly as Lord Zeus wants us to be. Think you he truly cherishes these Underworld reports?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I... hadn't considered that he might not. He seems the sort to want to know about all goings-on, especially ones dangerous to him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
