dialogueLines.text: Correct! He grows uneasy when the silence thickens. He'll get his prompt reports; though, just remember, he already has a messenger.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, I was wondering if you might accompany me to the springs for a short while? If you're not overly preoccupied?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, well, I do believe I could use a bit of a soak. I've more knots in my back than that tree! Let's be quick about it, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, but this is all right. Thank you for replenishing these waters! One of those details I had been meaning to get to, but... alas.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're very welcome, Headmistress. I appreciate your saying so, as I half-thought you would admonish me for using magick and resources on this sort of thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Magick mostly is for self-improvement, Melinoë. Not a shortcut, mind you. And if an occasional scalding-hot soak in the springs isn't self-improvement, then my concept of it must be very flawed.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wish I could bring myself to relax. I feel like I'm wasting precious time while I'm here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Time spent being good to oneself is never wasted... unless you overthink it overmuch! We are here. Seize the moment, ere it passes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: A shame we cannot cook indefinitely in those springs, but... a brief dip is better than none at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was thinking of putting these purifying salts to use, Headmistress. Call it a cleansing ritual. Would you accompany me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: A visit to the springs ought rid us of unwanted thoughts for a while. Let us be cleansed together, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Mm. We're like two nettles boiling in a cauldron, aren't we?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I remember bathing in this spring when I was very small. You would ask me never to splash your hair, and I would do it anyway. You were so tolerant of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, now at least you know the reason that your training was so rigorous! There came a point when even my vast tolerance for all your splashing finally wore thin.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wait... you trained me to the height of my ability as a witch because I got your hair wet?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Mm, was either that, or transform you into a newt, or something! Come off it, Melinoë. You are entirely too serious, sometimes.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Heh. A lesson I've been unable to take to heart, Headmistress. I suppose it's been a while since we talked like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, well, that's growing up for you. And present circumstances haven't helped, either. This blasted hot-spring got all covered up with moss and had been stagnant, I don't know how long. Being here again... it takes me back.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Do you think we'll be able to keep the moss from reclaiming this place again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll watch the moss, you watch the splashing. Deal?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Whew! Much better. Sometimes I think that hot-spring could raise the dead.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know it's a difficult time, Headmistress, but just in case, would you be interested in joining me for a brief while at the hot springs?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: As much as I would enjoy basking in the springs, I've incantations to complete, wards to reinforce.... No, I cannot. Not now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You had an audience with the Three Fates! Your incantation from so many nights ago must have taken effect. Hermes indicated they're in good spirits, but shall be continuing their leave. Well done drawing them out nevertheless.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Headmistress. I get the impression that they sought me more so than the other way around, but at least we know they're safe. They... also mentioned you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: They did, did they...? In what capacity?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They said you've... well... is there someplace more private where we can discuss this, do you think?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: <Sigh> Let's to the springs. You've earned the rest, and we can sit and talk about what's on your mind... and theirs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: So, what do the three weaving-sisters know of me, then, hm? You were about to say...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They told me... I believe the phrase they used was, you had pulled some strings. They didn't elaborate, but suggested I ask you about it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Pulled strings. I know full well what they meant. But to this point I was uncertain whether they did. Let me twist your question back; what do you suppose they implied?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I got the sense they felt that you had altered their designs somehow. Pulling strings... changing their weavings. And not in a small way either, but... I merely guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You don't merely anything, Melinoë. Well, if those three already know my past, you ought to know it, too. And 'tis easier to explain, I suppose, if you look upon me as I am.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, what... I... you're... you're beautiful... but what is this, I — I don't understand... some trick you're playing or some glamour, or...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: No. The trick is when the veil is on. Look upon me, and do not avert your eyes. I know those eyes better than you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm... you're... how is this possible...? Who are you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: When I was a mere babe, I washed up on the bank of the Cocytus with naught but moonlight to guide me. Selene looked out for me; informed me that the Titan Chronos took my family, and that I alone escaped. Thus I trained.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You trained... for the task. Death to Chronos. You attempted all of that... before I did?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Before is not entirely correct. I was a capable student, but very raw; vengeance was my headmistress. And one night, I found the wretch. Spewed every curse that I knew... and descended into the abyss of his subconscious.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But, I had you to teach me to descend to the abyss of the subconscious... you mean to say you figured it out entirely on your own? That's impossible!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Speaking from experience, I beg to differ. But at any rate, something did go awry. Time and nightmare fused, and I discovered I was someplace else. I sought the end of Time... but had arrived somewhere closer to the beginning.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And then, you lived... for aeons... waiting, for the weavings of the Fates and me? For a second chance. Headmistress, I... gods...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I dared not tamper with the Fates' designs more than was strictly needed. In this form, I ingratiated myself to your father and mother; became handmaiden to the Queen, in fact. Thus when Chronos inevitably struck... they gave their newborn daughter not to the River Styx, but to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then... you did everything in your power to prepare me, so that I might succeed where you failed...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Correct. 'Tis as I always told you, I am not your mother, Melinoë. Neither did I have one myself. However, I had reason to be confident in your abilities.... So, there you are! How you must hate me for all this...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No... how can you even say that still, Headmistress? I love you... and you are my mother, whether you can admit it or not. And even if... we're far too much alike.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I love you, too, of course, Melinoë, with all my heart and soul. Far more than I could ever love myself. And I am so sorry... for all that you had to endure. For the family you never had.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Nary a soul must know of this, I trust you understand? The Three Fates, fine; and we, and no one else. Now then... shall we pretend that none of this transpired?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: May moonlight guide you on your path and blind your enemies.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Death to Chronos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...heart of wormwood, earth-apple, spider silk, wax-stone...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...mulberry, snake fang, sage blossom, scorpion-tail...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Continue your task. Our enemy is distracted.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: May you cast a tall shadow in the light of the Moon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I leaden our enemies with the weight of my curse. So mote it be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You have returned to us, as has your strength. Go use it well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I know your task is difficult, and also know you have it in you to succeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Push yourself hard, Melinoë, but do not break.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Our enemy is distracted. Find him, and strike him down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You learn and grow each time that you set out. Continue on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Proceed at pace. No task of any consequence can be completed without effort.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Much left to do both on the surface and below...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: We witches oft are underestimated. But I am well aware how capable you are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Chronos is old enough to think that he has nothing left to learn, the utter fool.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Your power hinges on your confidence. Do not allow yourself to falter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Recover as you must. Returning thus is not a backward step.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not relent, Melinoë. Our enemy most certainly shall not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...moly petal, cypress leaf, mandrake root, quartz dust, garlic...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...moon-water, nettle, beetle-shell, nightshade...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...willow bark, sulfur, wing of bat, onion-skin...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The moon is new and offers us abundant possibility in what's to come.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Proceed as though your path is yours to choose; no one decides for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Had enough of the Underworld? The surface could surely use a witch's touch.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The Underworld always has need of you if the surface is well enough in hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: So many victories, you stand to forget the bitter taste of defeat altogether.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Accumulating victories as though they were reagents that you'd put into a pot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: One fewer remnant of the Father of All Monsters exists, as I surmise. Well done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Another possibility of Typhon, dead and gone. We heard the positive result.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: How strange it must be, having to confront old Chronos in his misconception of your father's House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Do give those remnants of old Chronos a curse or two on my behalf.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: We can but learn, Melinoë. Go on, and see what else this world has to teach.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Fear is palpable tonight. But you're no stranger to that sense by now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Time forced our hand, Melinoë. Nary a soul expected you to slay our enemy last night, on a new moon, no less!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I lack such forgiveness for my failure, Headmistress Hecate. The Titan yet lives, and Olympus yet burns, while I'm back where I started!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes; recovered bodily if not in spirit, and grown wiser for it. You've not failed... merely begun! This is all part of the task. Death to Chronos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: This cauldron's filthy still, alas. But there's something at our Altar you may use, and I've unsealed the Silver Pool as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: We shall await your safe return. May moonlight guide you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Twas a worthwhile first attempt, Melinoë. I trust you'd have gone farther still had I not obstructed your path. Quite unsuccessfully, I concede.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You needed to ensure I was prepared. And I know there is much left to prove.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Just so. Trust well your instincts and your craft, and continue the task. Death to Chronos.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Cocytus teems with life there by the riverbank again, Headmistress. Would you please join me for a look?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, very well, why not! I suppose I could use a little walk, and we could use the fish.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...'Twas not very long ago that you never would have permitted yourself this type of excursion. Nor had the mettle to request that I accompany you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, you're right. I could be hunting for our enemy this very moment, and you... you've so many responsibilities, yet I'm wasting your time. This was a mistake.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Hold, lest you frighten the river denizens; only then should my time be wasted here, Melinoë! You mistook the meaning of my words. Your capacity to still your racing mind is not a detriment. 'Tis a growing strength!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm... surprised you see it that way. Sometimes I feel I'm losing my focus. Why else would I have brought us here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you reasoned that we each need occupy our minds with other matters for a little while. And we are failing at it by continuing to speak! Let us be still together and direct our focus on the waters there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The river always provides, and let no such task ever be beneath us.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't suppose you'd be willing to head to the fishing pier with me again? Good place to occupy our minds with other matters for a little while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: That it can be. Then very well. There is aught I wish to speak with you about, besides.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You truly believe I wish to rid myself of you? Either that or you sought to wound me more deeply with your words than you're able to with the Nocturnal Arms.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm sorry, Headmistress, it's just... at the House, I realized I didn't just want my family back... I wanted you to be happy. But I felt like you were pushing me away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: My happiness is neither your responsibility nor is it within your grasp. And 'tis I who ought apologize. For all that I put you through. Forgive me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Never apologize when you are not at fault, you used to say. You were never easy on me, but also were never cruel. You taught me the difference, among so many things.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: A lack of cruelty is not the highest standard. At any rate, evidently I've taught you enough. I still shall challenge you in Erebus for sake of fitness and attunement, but in time, perhaps you shall refer to me not by title, but by name...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not as Headmistress, but as Hecate... that shall take a while. To think I used to use your name so casually when I was little. Much comes full circle with time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: More than you know. Now, let us shift our focus to the river denizens getting too close a listen for their own good...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: That was a pleasant enough outing, but speak not to me of happiness again. 'Tis a foolish thing with which to be concerned; a byproduct of fulfillment, nothing more.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Do you suppose we could take a brief break from all this and stop by the river over at the pier?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I do wish to see firsthand whether your skill at fishing has improved, yet unfortunately I have other matters that I must attend to now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, if it isn't inappropriate, I have a little gift. Seeing as your personal supply ran out a while back?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, the hounds shall lap this stuff right up I think! I shall safeguard it for them as a treat. And why don't you hold onto this, in kind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, come, Melinoë, you need not influence me with such gifts. We have a task. Let us stay focused on it, no?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You have taught me to apply myself to many things at once, Headmistress. Why can't I stay on task while paying my respects?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, well... I do have a bit of a taste for this stuff, I suppose. I once traveled much more often, to places where 'tis easier to procure. Perhaps you sensed in me some wistfulness about that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've learned from you, Headmistress. So, this is just a small token of my gratitude.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Whilst I appreciate the gesture, and the drink, 'tis entirely unneeded on your part. Have I not conditioned you to resist the temptations of unspoken contracts such as this?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know not to expect anything in return, Headmistress. The act of giving needs to be sufficiently rewarding in itself. And please believe me that it is, with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I've little taste for the traditions of the gods, you know. Lest you forget, I am a Titaness. More in common with our old enemy I daresay, than with the likes of Zeus and his ilk.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No matter your heritage, Headmistress, you have been a sure and steady guardian of mine throughout my life. A bit of tradition helps express the gratitude I feel.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Tradition is all well and good lest 'tis followed blindly; senselessly, I should say, for the blind are often very much aware. But let me not belabor your kind gesture; I am grateful, truly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought I said you needn't curry favor with me thus. I shall take this bottle, nonetheless; if but to penalize you for not offering it up instead to someone more deserving of your affection.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can think of no one else I would rather have this than you, Headmistress. You've made me the goddess I am, and not a night goes by that I'm not grateful for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: How often must I say, I'm not your mother, Melinoë. You needn't lavish me with such unbridled praise... I raised you to destroy our common foe. You owe no gratitude to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've often said, what we intend and what we do are not the same. I know you're not my birthmother, so what? You've cared for me; that's more than enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I've used you, and I'm using you still. Can't you see that? I've attempted not to hide this fact from you. Yet you turn it aside.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You have raised me in accordance with the promises you made to my father. He never asked you to coddle me, did he? Surely he wanted me to fend for myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: And do you think you can? You remain under my auspices, take aid from Olympus! You're not as independent as I planned. And furthermore, I've inadvertently caused you to think I've made you better off!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I believe you have. I know no other way. It fills me with sorrow, knowing you feel undeserving of the love I have for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Set your sorrow well aside for now. I'll drink your Nectar and enjoy it, if you please.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought I said you needn't curry favor with me thus. I shall take this bottle, nonetheless; if but to penalize you for not offering it up instead to someone more deserving of your affection.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can think of no one else I would rather have this than you, Headmistress. You've made me the goddess I am, and not a night goes by that I'm not grateful for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: How often must I say, I'm not your mother, Melinoë. You needn't lavish me with such unbridled praise... I raised you to destroy our common foe. You owe no gratitude to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've often said, what we intend and what we do are not the same. I know you're not my birthmother, so what? You've cared for me; that's more than enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I've used you to achieve a particular result, and not even the one I sought. Surely you see that? I've attempted not to hide this fact from you. Yet you turn it aside.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You have raised me in accordance with the promises you made to my father. He never asked you to coddle me, did he? Surely he wanted me to fend for myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, you do not! You remain under my auspices; take aid from Olympus! You may think for yourself, but remain dependent on many others. And furthermore, I've inadvertently caused you to think I've made you better off!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I believe you have. I know no other way. It fills me with sorrow, knowing you feel undeserving of the love I have for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Set your sorrow well aside for now. I'll drink your Nectar and enjoy it, if you please.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Why don't we head to the taverna once again? You honored me before, and I would dearly like to honor you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Then consider me honored. I've no desire to be worshiped thus; the Shades can leave their offerings for the hounds.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, you're the reason all of us are here. The Shades adore you, and wish to commemorate that this great task you devised is finally complete!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: We achieved what needed to be done, and we commemorated it last we attended the taverna. Now, hand over that Ambrosia, and I'll keep it for the next occasion.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You mentioned holding onto Ambrosia for the next celebratory occasion, so I wanted to donate more for when the time comes. To the next great victory of the Unseen!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, stop, Melinoë! Evidently neither of us knows what to do with themselves now, as you are so insistent on these most-unnecessary gifts! Leave me in peace.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I wasn't trying to upset you.... Did you truly wish for me to stay there in the House that much? With Mother and Father... where I belong, you always said.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Purge that poisoned thought at once. I taught you a great deal in some respects but to the detriment of your experience in other walks of life.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Through your achievements, our status as the Unseen grows. So long as we do not become the subject of too many tales, other than the frightful kind.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Our Shades all seem to understand the need for continued discretion. They seem quite excited Nyx is back and watching over us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: They shall discover Night Incarnate is not easy to please.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You've consistently ensured the safety of your Familiars, not that I had any doubt. We are here to protect such creatures more so than the other way around.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Familiars are family, Headmistress. Perhaps through this ordeal, my bond with mine shall come to be as strong as the one you share with yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Of that I have no doubt as well. I've discussed the matter with Hecuba and Gale both, and they are willing to watch over you. If you ask nicely, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What, truly? But they're bound to you! Wait, who must I ask nicely, you or them?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: One further secret of the Oath of the Unseen I shall reveal now. When you feel suitably prepared, consider this. Then perhaps we shall clash under a new set of circumstances...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The Nocturnal Arms you bear are strong, yet burden you with terrible responsibility. One that you are only beginning to understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The bearer shall uphold the will of Night in service to the Underworld. Almost as though I'm at their command and not the other way around...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: That is the correct way to look at it. And I would share with you a certain incantation that shall deepen this relationship of yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Got you, Hecate!! You're dead!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Haha, got me you did, Melinoë. But I daresay I can escape your clutches yet!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There you are! Got you again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Aah! So persistent! Give up already, Witch! For I'm a Titaness! You cannot vanquish me!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hahaha, I got you!! ...Hecate...? What's the matter?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Ah, it's merely that... tsk, I've not heard your laughter before. Reminded me of your mother! A much preferable sound to that of grief.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I promise not to cry again! I still get sad sometimes, but not as much...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not make promises you cannot keep. When tears well up, let them spill forth; they're not so precious that they need be contained.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But you never cry, Hecate! And I wish to become strong, like you! So that I might find Chronos, and destroy him for what he's done. And then you could hear my mother's laugh again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: And I shall do everything in my power to prepare you, so that you may succeed where I failed. If not for your family's sake, then for your birthright... as the Princess of the Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What do you mean you failed? You tried to beat Chronos yourself? He hurt you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Concern yourself with your own pain, not mine. Destroying Chronos shall not change the past, and the pain shall linger, though assert itself less frequently with age... but enough of this! On the morrow, we shall teach you to hunt greater prey.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Melinoë. I held back against you out there. And even still, you could not surpass me. Do you know why?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...I know I can grow stronger still, but... it's my lack of knowledge that betrayed me in the end. Some of your many tricks I've yet to learn.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Correct! Strength; knowledge; patience. When faced with a seemingly indomitable adversary, all three are required. Or a stroke of fate...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I thwarted you again out there. 'Tis unfair, I know. I arrive fresh as rain in anticipation of our clash, whilst you may already be battle-worn.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fairness is a luxury, Headmistress. Though, I do sometimes envy your ability to travel such long distances in an instant.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not be quick to envy what you do not understand. Along your path are to be found resources and reagents we require. And if you cannot get past me, then you are unprepared for what awaits beyond.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: How are you faring of late, Melinoë? You cannot hide your troubles from me forever, so I bid you reveal them at will.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm just... still thinking about our conversation at the springs, that's all. I know we mustn't speak of it, but... I understand now why you acted as you did while we were at the House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Very well. Then spare me your sympathies. I am fortunate beyond compare. Most of us do not get second chances to amend our failures.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've had innumerable chances. Night after night, attempting to achieve the same result.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You have not once failed. Only suffered temporary setbacks on your path to victory.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The pressure still is getting to you, even with the task essentially complete. 'Twas my desire to see you shake that burden from your shoulders by now. Why does it remain?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, each night I confront these remnants of old Chronos or Typhon. Such possibilities apparently are both real and innumerable, representing so many scenarios in which, for one reason or another, I failed...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Or merely have yet to succeed. I stand as living proof that you are not alone in what you aim to accomplish, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What are you saying, that there are others like you... like me? Attempting to achieve the same result?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I merely ask you to consider how the efforts you go to, the lengths you go to, may reverberate. The energy we put into this world comes back to us. Simply put, I am only here because you are.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How are you faring lately, Headmistress? You've been... well, more reserved than I expected since the House of Hades. You said we'd speak more later.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: How soon later turns to now. Look, Melinoë, I ought be in a more celebratory mood, given what you finally achieved. I am, above all, very proud of you. But being back at the House awakened memories that ought remain dormant.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Even though... you got to see Mother and Father again, and Nyx? They were so grateful to you, I could tell! I am so grateful to you. Things may not have gone precisely as we planned, but the result is what we sought, isn't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You refer to Chronos. I longed to see that wretch destroyed. My heart remains poisoned by the thought. I am attempting still to draw that poison out... do give me time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I remember you once told me that destroying Chronos would not change the past, and that the pain would linger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: And, I was probably correct. Yet we cannot help but reassess the turns our lives take, through our own choices and otherwise. Anyhow, are you prepared to carry on?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nearly so. Retracing my steps should be interesting...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: So is it working out thus far? This change of plan, as our Tactician would say. Should you see even the slightest trace of the old Chronos, do notify me at once.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Grandfather appeared where and when we discussed. His claims about these remnant possibilities out of Time appear to be accurate. Shall we continue?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: We may as well see where it leads. Also I wished to thank you, for your concern for me before. And express my gratitude that you decided to remain with us. I was... angry with your decision at first. But no longer.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It was not a difficult decision for me, Headmistress. It would have been harder had I realized it might anger you. I've been to both the Palace of Zeus and the House of Hades... and neither has a cauldron, or soft earth beneath my feet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand only too well, Melinoë, and I would have chosen the same, except... I thought you'd have been the wiser. Now you are stuck here, another lost soul in the crossroads 'twixt heaven and hell...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But I'm not lost. I'm where I wish to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress... I wanted to say, I'm sorry that you couldn't share in all those memories of the House of Hades... of how I might have grown up had Chronos chosen differently.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: No need for consolation, but thank you. You experienced a course in which my intervention was unnecessary. 'Twas for the best. I am gladdened you experienced such visions.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I just don't understand why you weren't there. It's odd to say, but... I think I always felt your absence, somehow. I never learned my craft... so much of what I hold dear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: But you have learned. As for that other possibility... it sounds as though the Titan proved unexpectedly useful around the House. Thus I was not there because your mother never needed me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Lest you be tempted otherwise, do keep the details of what transpired at the House of Hades closely guarded... such as what you now recall. Rumors of your command over dreams and time may spread, but not the truth.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Because we achieved what should not have been possible... but, all these memories I've gained... perhaps I can tell you more about them sometime, at least? And others with whom I'm close.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps, eventually... though for now, discretion's the priority by will of Night herself. You are saved some complicated explanations in so doing, as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Your magick may have proven instrumental to the reconciliation of your family recently, but it shall only take you and any of them so far. Consider how rigorously you trained... all shall need to put forth a similar effort now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All these inter-family relationships need be tended like a garden. But my relatives have always struggled to communicate and get along, haven't they? Father couldn't even maintain ties with the Olympians.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis not one of your family's greatest strengths, that much is true. And it may never become one. Nonetheless, we have seen the consequences of the mistakes of the past, and those ought never to recur. You'll see to it, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress? I've a bottle of Ambrosia here, and... thought perhaps that we could share it at the taverna, if you could step away with me a bit! I know it's an odd time...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Melinoë, I'm going to resist my impulse to tersely decline, so that I might remind myself as to whether the greatest of Olympian delicacies lives up to its reputation! Let's be off.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I had not given this taverna any real thought. Such gathering-places, where one can be unguarded... dare-I-say relaxed? A liability. We need our senses sharp.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But now that you're here, you've come to realize that having such a place where we and Shades can comingle has its benefits? And that I didn't overstep when I restored it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes I have, and no, you did not. The tension about these Crossroads has grown too palpable for my liking, as this much nervous energy does our cause no good. But, I digress. Discussion of our cause ought be off limits here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Ah yes, more rules! Just what this taverna needs to ease the tensions of our small band of loyalists. I think you're onto something, Headmistress!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohh, I see how it is. What, a sip or two of Ambrosia and suddenly your inhibitions are all gone? A powerful effect! We ought to use it in the cauldron if not here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...We're still talking about our cause, and our craft. Why don't you... tell me of a time when you were closer to my age, if you don't mind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I mind. Though perhaps as we delve deeper into this bottle, I shall not mind as much! Shall we find out? To discovering the truth!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Ignore my sullen turn back there, Melinoë. These evenings I am more concerned about the future than the past. I trust I did not overly affect the atmosphere.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Would you care to split this bottle of Ambrosia, Headmistress? We never truly celebrated the restoration of the Underworld's King and Queen here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Sipping the drink of your mountain relatives hardly seems suitable festivity, though very well. You are correct that these Shades shall be expecting something.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...And so, I bid you all salute your Princess of the Underworld, who never relented against the threat of Time itself, and the Father of All Monsters, amongst many others!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Headmistress. Everyone! I never could have done this without you... without these Crossroads, the one place I could always return to in a moment of need.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You are welcome here and safe, Melinoë. And whilst we had utmost faith in you, nevertheless what you accomplished was beyond imagining.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I did what you trained me to do. I'm grateful for your patience through all the many setbacks I had to overcome.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: How else could we have stood against Time itself if not through patience? I knew you would be a capable student. 'Tis the only reason I pushed you as hard as I did.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So then what happens now to the Unseen? Our Shades each have an honorary place in Elysium waiting for them...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: That may be, if they decide to leave. But, I suspect they're in no greater hurry to abandon this place than are you. There is always more we may accomplish here, and we stand to go farther together than alone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Well, 'twas not the most formal gathering, but my sense is that it was worthwhile? All these Shades... they arrived here for lack of anyplace to go. Now, 'tis a permanent home.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't suppose I could persuade you to come join me at the taverna for a bottle of Ambrosia, could I, Headmistress?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: No, I don't suppose you can, not now. I shan't darken the mood there amongst the Shades requiring respite.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You prevailed in your attempt! But our enemy is Time itself, and 'twould seem that he is not prepared to abdicate his stolen throne as yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I often fought back feelings that this task would be impossible. But we're getting somewhere, aren't we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Impossibility tends to be transient. Time would wear us down, but with patience, we can withstand him until our night arrives at last.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You have gone far, Melinoë, and you bested me. Gave me quite a scrape, at that! There is much left to be done. But, you are learning.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Headmistress. Although, I know that I could not have bested you had you held nothing back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: You know? Why do you self-impose such limits on your capability? Alter your thoughts. I shall see you again out there.
